title: Meet The Startup Summoning UFOs: Skywatcher Interview
summary: James Valor, founder of Skywatcher, discusses how his private technology company accidentally discovered UAPs during government war game exercises in 2021 and has since developed sophisticated detection systems. The interview covers their taxonomy of nine UAP classes, their experiences with various craft behaviors including being hit by energy weapons, and their interactions with government agencies who appear genuinely uncertain about the phenomena.

acronyms.definition: Broader term than UFO encompassing unidentified objects in the sky
Traditional term for unidentified objects in the sky
Term used to describe potential non-human origin of UAPs
Electromagnetic signals in the radio frequency spectrum used for detection
Legal agreement to keep information confidential
Collective term for US intelligence agencies
Secure room for discussing classified information

events.description: James Valor retired from military after 23 years as a sergeant major
Skywatcher team first observed Class 1 UAPs during a government war game exercise
First observation of Class 2 (tic tac) UAPs
Chinese spy balloon flew over Alaska and Montana ICBM detection sites
Nimitz UFO encounter with David Fravor and USS Princeton

key_claims.claim: UAPs literally come to Skywatcher day after day after they turn on their 'dog whistle' technology
Skywatcher has identified nine different classes of UAPs with distinct characteristics
Class 1 UAPs (tetras) flew 2-3 thousand feet overhead but were invisible to human senses while detectable by cameras
UAPs appear to probe and test Skywatcher's detection capabilities by flying through the effective range of their telemetry systems
A UAP hit Skywatcher's equipment with a microwave weapon, shutting down their systems
Class 2 tic tac UAPs are approximately 40 feet in length and appear to have spike-like protrusions
Class 2 tic tacs appear to bend when coming out of high-G maneuvers, suggesting flexible or semi-rigid construction
Government officials in classified settings have told Skywatcher they have no knowledge of what they're observing and suggested it may be off-planet
The government has advanced secret physics and knowledge probably since at least the end of World War II
Skywatcher has five years of UAP data including terabytes of radar, imagery, video, photos, and signals intelligence
Jake Barber was one of the first people to retrieve a craft of non-human origin, an egg-shaped craft
Skywatcher has been contracted by the government to collect UAP data under contracts ostensibly for war games
